dynamic_line: Greetings. Not many people wandering around these days.
Hello there. Bit surprised to see a civilian here.
Welcome to what's left of RAF Lakenheath... or what used to be RAF Lakenheath.
Careful where you step. Security's not what it used to be.
Flight Lieutenant Davies, Royal Air Force. Or what's left of it.
Squadron Leader Thompson. I used to fly Typhoons before... well, before all this.
Wing Commander Harris. Not that rank means much these days.
Just a pilot without a plane. Name's Mitchell.

responses.text: How did you end up here?

dynamic_line: Was stationed here when everything went to hell. Most personnel evacuated or... well, they didn't make it.
I was on a training flight when the first reports came in. By the time I landed, half the base was overrun.
Flew in from Brize Norton on one of the last transport flights. Supposed to be temporary. That was weeks ago.
Was doing a joint exercise with the Americans. Then communications went dark, and those things started appearing.
You have no idea. Watching those things swarm across the runway... I still have nightmares.
It was. But we're trained to keep our heads in a crisis. Some of us made it through.
Nothing in flight school prepares you for the end of the world.
I've seen some things I wish I could forget. But dwelling on it doesn't help anyone.
Same thing that happened everywhere else. Those things showed up, people panicked, discipline broke down.
It was chaos. We tried to evacuate personnel and essential equipment, but it happened too fast.
Command ordered an evacuation. Some aircraft got out. The rest of us... well, we're still here.
We held out longer than most bases, but eventually we were overrun. A few of us barricaded ourselves in here.
A few. Mostly ground crew and security personnel. They're out on patrol right now.
Not many. Most of the pilots evacuated with their aircraft. Those of us without planes were left behind.
There were more, but we've lost people to those things, to disease, to... well, despair.
Just a handful now. We take shifts keeping watch.
Afraid not. The last operational aircraft left weeks ago. The ones that remained were damaged or out of fuel.
You're joking, right? If I had a working aircraft, do you think I'd still be here?
There's a Typhoon in the hangar, but no fuel and the electronics are fried. Just a very expensive paperweight now.
Nothing that will fly. We've cannibalized most of them for parts to keep the generators running.
Tell me about it. I'm a pilot without a plane. Not much use these days.
What did you expect? That we'd just hop in a fighter jet and fly off into the sunset?
Life's full of disappointments. Getting used to that is key to surviving these days.
Even if we had aircraft, where would we go? Last radio contact suggested it's the same everywhere.
